chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI CP D 1877 of 2025 Date Order with Signature of Judge(s) Hearing For hearing of main case. 14-05-2025 Mr. Ramiz Nasim, advocate for the petitioner Ms. Alizeh Bashir, Deputy Attorney General along with Mr. Muhammad Akbar Khan, Assistant Attorney General DSP Ayaz Ahmed, FRO/SB, Karachi SIP Javed Akhtar, FRO/SB, Karachi SI Muhammad Aftab, FIA Investigation, Karachi. Comments filed on behalf of respondents No.2 & 4 and 3 & 5 are taken on record. The plea taken by the respective respondents is as follows:- Respondents No.2 and 4 It is therefore humbly requested that kindly be passed the orders in the larger interest of justice. 1. To allow to verify their travel documents through Ministry of foreigner affairs for confirmation of their genuineness. 2. To allow to verify their status, criminal record CRO / CRI accordingly because they are illegally staying in Pakistan since long. 3. To direct D.G NADRA Sindh to verify the petitioner through Bio- Matric for NADRA registration, if petitioners register in NADRA, their CNIC's may be canceled/Blocked and also provide clearance certificate to petitioners. 4. To allow, to deport to their native country, after completing legal and required formalities under the foreigner Act 1946. Sd/- Foreigner Registration Officer, Special Branch, Karachi. (On behalf of respondent No.02 & 05) Respondents No.3 and 5 It is respectfully submitted that previously, the Deportees, who produced lawful requisite documents at FIA, Immigration Counter Departure, Karachi have already travelled to Bangladesh. Further submitted that, there is no delay on the part of the answering respondents for the travelling abroad / deportation of the petitioners.

Sd/- Foreigner Registration Officer, Special Branch, Karachi. (On behalf of respondent No.02 & 05) Respondents No.3 and 5 It is respectfully submitted that previously, the Deportees, who produced lawful requisite documents at FIA, Immigration Counter Departure, Karachi have already travelled to Bangladesh. Further submitted that, there is no delay on the part of the answering respondents for the travelling abroad / deportation of the petitioners. However, it is pertinent to mention here that the following documents are required at the time of departure on deportation. i. Travel Permits issued from the High Commission of Bangladesh. ii. Order from the concerned Home Department iii. Letter from Foreign Registration Office, Special Branch, Karachi having the request for their deportation iv. Travel permit issued by Special Branch v. A letter issued from Additional Inspector General Police to Foreign Registration Office. vi. Exit Permit In view of the above, whenever any of the petitioner will appear at FIA Immigration Counter along with lawful requisite documents, the individual will be allowed to proceed abroad as per law subject to any conduction. Sd/- (MUHAMMAD AFTAB) SUB INSPECTOR FIA, I&AHS, JIAP, Karachi For & on behalf of Respondent No.03&04 Learned counsel for the petitioner states that the petitioner would be satisfied if the petition is disposed of in the aforesaid terms. Order accordingly. Judge Judge Ayaz Gul
